Question: What was the size of the force that held off the Persian army at Thermopylae?

generation: The size of the force held off the Persian army at Thermopylae is a matter of historical debate and varies depending on the source. However, most historians agree that it was likely around 7,000-10,000 Greeks, led by King Leonidas of Sparta, who fought against an estimated 100,000-300,000 Persian soldiers during the Battle of Thermopylae in 480 BCE.
